steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he ran) to a doer noun (someone who runs) by adding alif after first letter
Added ات to make it refer to multiple female runners
Added ال to point to specific runners being discussed

steps.m: and the female ones running swiftly (being sworn by in an oath)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at start + special ending sound to show it's being used in an oath

steps.m: to pant/breathe heavily (especially of horses running)

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun showing the sound/action of horses breathing heavily while running
Added ًا at the end to make it describe the action/sound of horses breathing heavily while running

steps.m: to kindle fire, to strike sparks

steps.n: Adding م prefix and vowels creates active participle meaning 'one who does the action'

steps.m: female ones who produce fire (acted on)
steps.n: Adding -aat ending to make it about multiple female things that make sparks (like horses' hooves striking rocks)

steps.m: the female ones who produce fire
steps.n: Making the word definite with the article ال

steps.m: then by those (horses) that strike sparks (of fire)
steps.n: This refers to war horses striking sparks with their hooves. The word is in genitive case (ِ ending) because it follows an oath. The alif in ال is written as ٱ when preceded by فَ.

steps.m: to strike or hit something to produce sparks

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'one instance of striking'. This word describes what the war horses (الموريات) are doing.
Added ًا (an) at the end to show this is the type of striking the war horses are doing - it's like saying 'a striking' instead of just 'striking'

steps.m: to raid, attack suddenly, or make a swift assault

steps.n: Added alif maksura (ٰ) after ر which is common in Quranic spelling
Adding ات at the end makes this word refer to multiple female raiders instead of just one raider
Adding ٱلْ makes the word definite

steps.m: then/so the female ones who raid
steps.n: Adding فَ as a conjunction prefix

steps.m: then/so the female ones who raid (subject of صُبْحًا)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this is the subject (doer) of the action that happens in the morning (صُبْحًا)
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter to change from action to time

steps.m: in the morning (describing when فَٱلْمُغِيرٰتِ/the charging horses happen)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end shows this word describes when something happens. Here it tells us when the charging horses (فَٱلْمُغِيرٰتِ) make their raid.

steps.m: to stir up or raise dust

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at beginning makes the meaning causative - doing something to make something else happen
The و drops out and نَ is added to show multiple feminine subjects (referring to the horses mentioned earlier)

steps.m: and then they (f) stirred up (the dust نَقْعًا)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens after something else. The word نَقْعًا after it means 'dust'
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added the pronoun ه with kasra (هِ) to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the dust being stirred up.

steps.m: with/by it/him (referring to the dust being stirred up)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it mean the dust itself rather than the action of stirring up dust. This word is what is being stirred up by the horses in the verse.

steps.m: dust (object of أَثَرْنَ - they stirred up)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being stirred up by the horses in the verse

steps.m: to penetrate through or enter into the middle

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he went through/penetrated'
The ف prefix indicates sequence/consequence ('and then', 'so')
Changed the last vowel to a silent letter (sukun) to connect with another ending

steps.m: and then they (feminine plural) penetrated through
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show multiple women did the action in the past
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning with/by
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'it' (masculine). This word connects to جَمْعًا (gathering) in this ayah, showing what was gathered with.
Added small ي (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide to show this is masculine

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring together

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of gathering
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object (the thing being gathered)
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that mankind is truly ungrateful to their Lord.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
steps.n: The root letters form the base meaning related to human companionship
Changes from a verb about being social into a noun meaning humans (who are social beings)
Adds ان to create the common word for a single human being
Adds ال to specify we're talking about humans in general

steps.m: the human being (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Adds َ at the end to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence

steps.m: to nurture and care for something completely
steps.n: The root conveys the idea of lordship and nurturing care
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or ownership
Added هِ to show it belongs to him (mankind). The small ۦ after هِ is just for pronunciation.

steps.m: to deny blessings or be ungrateful
steps.n: The root represents the concept of ingratitude
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely true. This word is describing 'mankind' (الإنسان) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added tanwin (double damma) at the end to show this is a general description, not referring to a specific person
This is a common particle used for emphasis and confirmation
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Added هُ at the end which means 'he'. This word is connected to لَشَهِيدٌ (witness) later in the ayah - together they mean 'he is surely a witness'
This is the basic form of the word that means being in a higher position
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing position above something
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it points to something farther away
Added لِكَ to make it more specific and formal. This form is used when referring back to something already mentioned.

steps.m: to witness or be present at something
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of witnessing or testifying
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'

steps.m: one who witnesses (like a witness in court)
steps.n: Changed from past tense 'witnessed' to a noun meaning 'witness' by adding ي in the middle
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any witness, not a specific one
Added لَ at start to emphasize - means 'truly/indeed'
This is a common particle used for emphasis. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show strong emphasis.
The و connects this phrase to what came before it
The هُۥ shows who the statement is about. This word connects to لَشَدِيدٌ (intense/strong) later in the ayah to mean 'he is indeed intense in his love of wealth'.
Changes from verb 'to love' to the noun 'love'
Added لِ at the beginning to show 'for/to' and kasra at the end because it's followed by الْخَيْرِ (wealth/good things)
Root letters get vowels to make a simple noun meaning 'good/goodness'
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the wealth') and ِ to show it belongs to حُبِّ (love of)
Changes from verb to adjective showing strong quality
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the')
Adding لَ at start adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'
Starting with the basic negation particle لا
Added فَ to show this follows from what came before

steps.m: Then do they not (before يَعْلَمُ - know)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make this a question. This questions why they don't know what comes next in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to show present tense, sukun on ل to make it easier to pronounce, and ُ at the end to show it's doing the action. This verb is followed by إِذَا (when) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a point in time

steps.m: when (referring to بُعْثِرَ - things are scattered)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes it from referring to past time to future time. In this ayah, it's talking about when the graves will be turned inside out.

steps.m: to scatter or turn things upside down
steps.n: This is a four-letter root meaning to scatter things around
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: is scattered/overturned (referring to ما فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ - what is in the graves)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that the action is happening to something (in this case, the contents of graves) rather than someone doing the action
This is a question word used to ask about things. In this ayah, it's asking about what is in the graves.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows what is inside the graves.
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This is the standard spelling when the word stands alone.
This root is about burying something in the ground
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun for a single grave
Changed from single grave (قَبْر) to plural graves (قُبُور) by changing the middle vowels and adding و
Added ٱلْ to make it specific graves, and ِ because it comes after 'what is in' (مَا فِى) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels and added shadda to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: and was gathered thoroughly (what is in الصُّدُورِ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what came before. This word describes what happens to what is in الصُّدُورِ (the breasts/hearts)
This is a word that asks about or refers to things. Here it's asking about what is in people's hearts/chests (ٱلصُّدُورِ).
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word connects to الصُّدُورِ (the chests) in this ayah to show what is inside them.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Changes from singular 'chest' to plural by changing the middle vowels to damma (u sound)
Added ال with shadda on ص to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in) in the ayah
Added shadda to strengthen the emphasis. This particle introduces and emphasizes the statement that follows about their Lord (رَبَّهُم) being well-acquainted with them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
The extra mark (شدّة) on ب shows this means 'complete caretaker/nurturer' rather than just 'caring for'
Added هُم at the end to show possession. This word is the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or relates to something
Added the pronoun هِمْ (them) to show who the preposition relates to. This word connects to خَبِيرٌۢ (All-Aware) in the ayah, showing that Allah is All-Aware about/of them.
Connected the root letters together to make the basic word for 'day'
The word 'day' combines with 'idh' (meaning 'that') to make 'on that day'

steps.m: to inform, to be aware of something's true nature

steps.n: Root letters got vowels added to make past tense verb 'he informed/knew'
Changes the verb into an adjective showing someone who has deep knowledge or awareness
Adds emphasis meaning 'truly/indeed/certainly'
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's in the nominative case
